name: Ajanta Caves
description: Ancient Buddhist rock-cut cave monuments dating back to the 2nd century BCE. Famous for spectacular murals and sculptures. A UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Ellora Caves
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring 34 rock-cut caves representing Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain monuments from 600–1000 CE.

name: Bibi Ka Maqbara
description: Known as the Taj of the Deccan, this beautiful 17th-century Mughal mausoleum was built by Prince Azam Shah in memory of his mother.

name: Daulatabad Fort
description: One of the strongest forts in India's history, built on a conical hill with a 180-degree moat and labyrinthine passages.

name: Panchakki (Water Mill)
description: A scenic 17th-century water mill powered by an underground stream. Part of the Sufi shrine complex, surrounded by beautiful gardens and a serene lake.

name: Grishneshwar Temple
description: One of the 12 revered Jyotirlinga shrines of Lord Shiva in India. An important pilgrimage destination with ancient architecture.
